cross_scene_arcs.title: From Grief to Vengeance: The Unraveling of Frank's Death
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carter's journey begins in mourning but evolves into a relentless pursuit of truth and justice after discovering inconsistencies in his brother Frank's death. What starts as personal grief transforms into a targeted investigation, culminating in the revelation that Frank was murdered as part of a larger conspiracy, shifting Carter's goal from understanding to vengeance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carter is emotionally suspended between life and death, entangled in grief and ritual.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carter is assassinated while attempting to walk away, his quest for vengeance unfulfilled but his role as a threat neutralized.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full conspiracy behind Frank and Doreen's murders remains unexposed, and Carter dies without achieving closure or justice.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Brother Bond and the Weight of the Past
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carter's relationship with his brother Frank is defined by absence, estrangement, and unresolved tension. From covertly reentering his home to retrieving Frank's ashes and ultimately discovering the truth of his murder, Carter's actions are driven by a need to reconcile with a brother he never fully connected with in life.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carter enters his home like a stranger, symbolizing emotional distance from his family and brother.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carter discovers Frank was murdered and that his niece Doreen was a victim, transforming his emotional detachment into violent resolve.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The true nature of Carter and Frank's relationship—its conflicts, affections, and missed reconciliations—remains unspoken and unhealed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confrontation with Power: From Outsider to Threat
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carter transitions from a passive mourner to an active disruptor of a powerful criminal network. His escalating confrontations with Kinnear, Thorpe, Brumby, and Gerald's agents mark a series of power struggles, each pushing him deeper into direct conflict with entrenched authority until he becomes a target to be eliminated.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carter enters the antagonists' space uninvited, asserting himself as a visible but isolated challenger.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carter is assassinated from a distance, his defiance ending in silent execution by unseen forces.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The network that orchestrated his brother's death remains intact, and Carter's death goes unavenged, leaving the power structure unchallenged.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Intimacy as Control and Escape
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carter's intimate relationships—with Edna and Anna—are marked by intensity, control, and vulnerability. These moments of closeness serve both as emotional respite and as points of exposure, ultimately leading to his capture and near-destruction, revealing how intimacy becomes both a weapon and a weakness.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carter initiates a romantic-physical advance toward Edna, seeking connection through raw desire.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carter is caught in a vulnerable moment with Edna, leading to his recapture and loss of autonomy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The authenticity of Carter's emotional connections remains ambiguous—whether they are genuine or tools of manipulation or self-soothing under duress.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Supernatural Trap: Agency vs. Predestination
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Carter's investigation increasingly intersects with a hidden, possibly magical order. His arrival at the 'demon king's' domain, the orchestrated revelations, and his ultimate assassination suggest he was not merely pursuing justice but fulfilling a predetermined role in a larger, unseen design that eroded his free will.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carter arrives at the multi-storey car park under Glenda's guidance, entering a space that feels fated.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Carter is killed while discarding his weapon, his attempt to exit the narrative thwarted by forces that treated him as a disposable agent.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains unclear whether Carter's actions were truly his own or part of a magical or systemic manipulation that rendered him a pawn in a ritualistic power structure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Shadow to Adversary: Brumby's Unmasking and Escalation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Cliff Brumby evolves from an offscreen conspirator into Carter's direct antagonist through a series of escalating confrontations, culminating in Brumby assassinating Carter. Each encounter intensifies their conflict, shifting from indirect exposure to physical violence and ultimately fatal betrayal.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Brumby is an unnamed, offscreen figure implicated in Carter's attempted elimination.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Brumby becomes the instrument of Carter's death, acting on behalf of Kinnear's conspiracy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full scope of Brumby's allegiance and whether he acted solely under Kinnear's orders remains ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Patriarch: Brumby's Domestic Tyranny
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Brumby's controlling and exploitative relationships with his daughter Sandra and with Glenda reveal a pattern of patriarchal dominance and moral corruption, positioning him as a figure who exerts power through fear, money, and surveillance within both family and illicit networks.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Brumby violently disrupts his daughter's party, asserting authoritarian control.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: His transactional and voyeuristic relationship with Glenda is exposed, deepening the portrait of his predatory behavior.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term consequences of his abuse on Sandra and Glenda, and whether they will resist or escape his influence, remain unexplored.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Failed Alliance: Brumby's Bid for Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Brumby attempts to reposition himself from antagonist to strategic ally by offering Carter intelligence on Kinnear, but his efforts collapse due to Carter's distrust and Brumby's own contradictory actions, ultimately leading to his violent rejection and the failure of his survival strategy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Brumby proposes a temporary alliance with Carter to eliminate a mutual threat.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: His attempt to expose corruption backfires, resulting in physical assault and complete estrangement from Carter.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Brumby genuinely sought justice or manipulated the situation to serve his own ends remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Puppeteer of Exploitation: Brumby's Hidden Empire
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Brumby's influence extends across illicit ventures—from property development to pornography and political blackmail—revealing a hidden status as a key node in a corrupt network. His power is maintained through surveillance, financial control, and strategic violence, culminating in his role as an assassin.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Brumby is revealed as a financier of Glenda's apartment and consumer of exploitative media.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He executes a high-stakes assassination, confirming his operational role in the conspiracy's enforcement wing.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full structure of the network Brumby serves, and whether he was a master or a pawn, remains obscured.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Unraveling of a Hidden Past
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eric's attempts to conceal his past gradually collapse as key figures recognize and expose his ties to Kinnear's criminal network, culminating in the full revelation of his role in Doreen's exploitation and Frank's murder.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eric is living under a fragile disguise, attempting to remain anonymous at the racetrack.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eric is publicly and definitively exposed as a central figure in the criminal operation, directly responsible for grave acts.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moral ambiguity of Eric's complicity—whether he acted under coercion or with full agency—remains unexplored.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rise and Fall in the Power Hierarchy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eric oscillates between marginalization and recognition within the criminal hierarchy, briefly gaining favor with Cyril and Kinnear before withdrawing from the center of power, signaling internal or strategic shifts.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eric is publicly diminished after his past affiliation with Kinnear is revealed.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eric disengages from the party at The Heights, physically and symbolically stepping back from the power structure.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The true nature of the arrangement discussed by Kinnear and whether it represented advancement or entrapment remains unclear.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Escalation of Conflict with Carter
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eric's antagonistic relationship with Carter evolves from covert recognition to open warfare, marked by psychological warfare, betrayal, and a final, fatal confrontation on the beach.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Carter recognizes Eric at the racetrack, initiating a personal and dangerous confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eric is overpowered, tortured, and killed by Carter, his body disposed of at sea.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite Eric's death, the emotional and moral cost of Carter's vengeance remains unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Facilitator to Fugitive
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eric transitions from a position of control and authority—overseeing race outcomes and group movements—to a desperate fugitive fighting for survival as his crimes catch up with him.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eric is in control, driving the group and managing the aftermath of the race.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eric is physically broken, unconscious, and on the verge of death after a failed escape.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Eric ever intended to escape permanently or was merely delaying the inevitable remains ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Web of Hidden Connections
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eric's network of relationships—with Albert, Kinnear, Gerald, and Cyril—reveals him as a linchpin in a larger criminal ecosystem, where loyalty, information, and betrayal are strategically exchanged.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eric is referenced indirectly by the Girl in the cafe, suggesting quiet influence.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eric is summoned by Kinnear and involved in high-level communications, indicating embeddedness in the organization.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The depth of Eric's personal alliances and whether any of these figures would protect or sacrifice him remains uncertain.
